title: 2) Dermis
text:
The dermis is directly related to aging and divided into two layers, the upper papillary
layer and the lower reticular layer. The papillary layer is composed of loose connective tissue
and  is  responsible  for  ﬁngerprints.  The  reticular  layer  is  composed  of  dense,  irregular
connective tissue and gives the skin strength and elasticity. It contains collagen and elastic
ﬁbers. The dermis also contains nerves, sensory receptors, blood vessels, hair follicles, oil
glands, sweat glands, and the Hyaluronic acid.
3)Hypodermis
The hypodermis called the subcutaneous layer, is the lower layer and not actually a part
of the skin. It consists mostly of adipose cells, which function as mechanical and thermal
insulators, macrophages, blood vessels, and nerves.
